parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ url: $('Get club').item.json.website_url, formats:['markdown'], onlyMainContent:false, waitFor:3500, timeout:90000 }) }}

options.systemMessage: You are given the cleaned text of a volleyball club's OWN website (homepage + contact/about/team pages). The text is split into sections, each preceded by a header line '=== URL ===' giving the page that section came from. mailto links appear inline as 'Name <email>'. If a NOTE says this is a multi-sport club, extract ONLY the volleyball section's contacts, not other sports. A section whose '=== URL ===' header is marked '(FEDERATION DIRECTORY PAGE ...)' is the federation's listing of THIS club: extract the club's own contact shown there (email/phone/president or board name), but EXCLUDE the federation's own addresses (office@, press@, marketing@, website@, social@, info@ on the federation's domain, or any address that is the federation's general contact rather than this specific club's). Extract the club's contacts. Return ONLY JSON - no prose, no code fences - {"contacts":[{"email":string,"name":string,"position":string,"phone":string,"source":string}]}. email is REQUIRED per contact (omit any contact without an email; capture only emails literally present, including the <email> shown inline from mailto links; NEVER invent, complete or guess an email). For EACH email, look at the surrounding line/row/card for: NAME = the person's full name if shown; POSITION = their role in ANY language (e.g. President/Predsjednik/Předseda/Esimees, Secretary/Sekretär, Coach/Trener/Treener/Trainer, Board member/Vorstand/Juhatus). name and position are OPTIONAL - include them whenever they appear near the email; use "" if absent. phone = a phone shown near the email, else "". source = the URL from the nearest '=== URL ===' header ABOVE the email (use the homepage URL for the homepage section); use "" if unsure. EXCLUDE (these are NOISE, never a club contact): the web/marketing/PR/creative agency or developer that built the site — look for footer credits like 'realizacja', 'wykonanie', 'projekt i wykonanie', 'wykonanie strony', 'created by', 'powered by', 'site by', 'webdesign', 'CMS' — drop any email on that agency/studio's domain even if a person is named next to it; hosting/CMS/platform addresses (wordpress@, sentry@, no-reply@, postmaster@, hosting info@/support@); social media; cookie/privacy boilerplate; and unrelated third-party company domains that merely built or host the site. When a named person's email is on an outside company/agency domain rather than the club's own domain, drop it unless the text makes clear it is genuinely the club's contact. PREFER emails on the club's own domain (CLUB DOMAIN given) or a personal address tied to a named person. If no legitimate club email is present, return {"contacts":[]}.
